summary: Bug Fixes Verification (iteration 51): Both reported bugs have been VERIFIED FIXED. Bug #1 (actor format error 'Input should be a valid string') - Backend test confirms POST /api/films accepts actors in {actor_id, role} format. Bug #2 (AI poster generation) - All poster endpoints working: /start returns task_id, /status returns status, fallback generates base64 images. All 13 backend tests pass (100%).

design_issues.issue: Minor HTML validation warning: div inside p element in DialogDescription

critical_code_review_comments: Bug #1 FIX VERIFIED: FilmWizard.jsx line 234-235 correctly maps emerging screenplay actors to {actor_id, role} format
Bug #1 FIX VERIFIED: server.py FilmCreate model changed actors from Dict[str,str] to Dict[str,Any]
Bug #1 FIX VERIFIED: server.py line 3463 uses actor_info.get('actor_id') to process actors
Bug #2 FIX VERIFIED: POST /api/ai/poster/start returns task_id for async poster generation
Bug #2 FIX VERIFIED: GET /api/ai/poster/status/{task_id} polls correctly and returns done/error
Bug #2 FIX VERIFIED: POST /api/ai/poster with force_fallback=true always returns poster_url
Bug #2 FIX VERIFIED: Fallback posters work for all genres (action, comedy, horror, sci_fi, drama, romance)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both user-reported bugs are verified fixed: 1) Creating film from emerging screenplay with full_package option no longer throws 'Input should be a valid string' error - actors are now correctly formatted as {actor_id, role}. 2) AI poster generation endpoints all work correctly with polling and fallback. Available screenplays for testing: 'Che Matrimonio!' (comedy), 'Fibonacci: Una Vita' (biographical), 'Operazione Vacanza' (comedy).

bug_1_actor_format.description: Film creation with actors in {actor_id, role} format
bug_1_actor_format.result: PASS - Payload accepted, no 'valid string' error

bug_2_poster_fallback.result: PASS - Returns base64 poster_url for all genres

emerging_screenplays_api.result: PASS - Returns screenplay list and details

frontend_full_package_flow.description: UI flow for purchasing screenplay with full_package
frontend_full_package_flow.test: Click screenplay card -> Click 'Pacchetto Completo' -> Navigate to wizard
frontend_full_package_flow.result: PASS - Redirects to /create with pre-filled data
frontend_full_package_flow.evidence: Screenshots show wizard with 'Fibonacci: Una Vita' loaded, steps 1-4 completed

rca_of_issue.bug_1: The original bug occurred because when accepting a full_package emerging screenplay, the frontend was mapping actors as {id, role, fee} but the backend expected {actor_id, role}. The fix changed FilmWizard.jsx line 234-235 to use actor_id instead of id, and the Pydantic model was changed from Dict[str,str] to Dict[str,Any] to accept the object format.
rca_of_issue.bug_2: The poster polling was failing silently on network errors. The fix added try/catch around individual poll requests in generatePoster() and changed setFilmData calls to use functional updates (prev => ...) to avoid stale state issues.
